## Task: Fix [medium] Code Quality: Comments reference deleted test files and a removed characterization suite (stale docs)
## Step: write-tests

### src/test-utils/mock-response.ts
```typescript
/**
 * Builds a minimal fetch `Response` stand-in for the mocked global `fetch`.
 *
 * `text()` returns the raw text (defaults to the JSON-stringified body).
 * `json()` returns the parsed body when one is provided; otherwise it falls
 * back to parsing `text` as JSON (when only `text` was given), and finally to
 * an empty object. `ok` is derived from `status` (true for any 2xx) unless an
 * explicit `ok` is supplied, in which case it wins.
 *
 * The `json()` method prefers an explicit `body` when supplied. When only
 * `text` was given, it parses the text as JSON; when neither is supplied it
 * falls back to an empty object `{}`. (No call site actually relies on this
 * branch: on the error path `ApiClient.request` only reads `.text()`, and on
 * the success path a `body` is always supplied.) The behavior is pinned by the
 * `mockResponse helper` characterization suite in `src/api.test.ts`.
 *
 * Test-only utility: deliberately kept out of the production `tsc` build via
 * the `src/test-utils/**` glob in `tsconfig.json`'s `exclude` array, so it is
 * never shipped to `wwwroot/dist`. Only test files import it.
 */
export interface MockResponseOptions {
  ok?: boolean;
  status?: number;
  body?: unknown;
  text?: string;
}

export function mockResponse(opts: MockResponseOptions): Response {
  const status = opts.status ?? 200;
  const ok = opts.ok ?? (status >= 200 && status < 300);
  const text = opts.text ?? JSON.stringify(opts.body ?? {});
  return {
    ok,
    status,
    text: async () => text,
    json: async () => opts.body ?? (opts.text ? JSON.parse(opts.text) : {}),
  } as unknown as Response;
}

```
You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: Code Quality
Severity: medium
File: src/test-utils/mock-response.ts:13-16, src/copy-assets.test.ts:5-8,24, src/ignore-patterns.test.ts:14-16
Title: Comments reference deleted test files and a removed characterization suite (stale docs)

Finding:
This refactor deleted `src/app.css.test.ts`, `src/readme.test.ts`, and the `mockResponse helper` characterization suite that used to live at the bottom of `src/api.test.ts`, but several doc comments still reference them as if they exist. (1) `src/test-utils/mock-response.ts` ends its doc block with "The behavior is pinned by the `mockResponse helper` characterization suite in `src/api.test.ts`." but that suite was removed and there is now NO direct characterization of `mockResponse` anywhere (verified: no test describes it). This is actively misleading: a maintainer will believe `mockResponse` is covered when it is not. (2) `src/copy-assets.test.ts` lines ~6-7 say it is pinned to node "mirroring `src/app.css.test.ts` and `src/readme.test.ts`" and line ~24 refers to "the parallel `app.css.test.ts`"; both files are deleted. (3) `src/ignore-patterns.test.ts` lines ~15-16 say it uses "the same approach `src/app.css.test.ts` and `src/readme.test.ts` use"; both deleted. Stale references to deleted files will confuse future readers and obscure the real coverage gap.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
Update the stale doc comments to stop referencing deleted files/suites. (a) In `src/test-utils/mock-response.ts`, replace the sentence "The behavior is pinned by the `mockResponse helper` characterization suite in `src/api.test.ts`." with a note that no dedicated characterization suite exists anymore (the `ok`/`text`/`json` branches are exercised only indirectly through the ApiClient and app tests), so any change to these fallbacks should be made deliberately. (b) In `src/copy-assets.test.ts`, remove the "(mirroring `src/app.css.test.ts` and `src/readme.test.ts`)" clause (lines ~6-7) and the "which the parallel `app.css.test.ts` reads at load time" phrase (line ~24), leaving the self-contained rationale that these tests spawn a child process / read files from disk and need no DOM. (c) In `src/ignore-patterns.test.ts`, remove the "(the same approach `src/app.css.test.ts` and `src/readme.test.ts` use for static files)" clause (lines ~15-16). Do not add back any reference to the deleted files.
